data.description:
# op-cert: producer signing seam — gates mint Ed25519 op-certs with the environment key (expand phase)

## Why
The two gate producers still sign verdicts with the legacy per-clone HMAC secret: plan-review via
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` `sign_plan_review()` → `signing.sign_manifest(kind="plan-review")`,
and the close gate via `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (`_signing.sign_manifest(kind="completion-verifier")`
after the material-drift recheck). The epic's design resolution ("Producer-signing & compute-runtime
design resolution") settles fork A — **uniform producer signing**: the producers themselves mint
`rebar.opcert.v1` DSSE certs with the ambient environment's Ed25519 key, so a local run and a
trusted-server run produce the same artifact and there is exactly ONE signature per verdict (no
double-signing; the remote handler never signs separately). This story is the expand phase:
write-new (Ed25519 op-certs), read-both (envelopes + legacy HMAC records). The storage keystone
(e4df, change 660) already gives `signing.sign_opcert_manifest()` an envelope-bearing `SIGNATURE`
event and `opcert.opcert_from_record()` read-back.

## What
- **Environment key genesis** (`src/rebar/signing.py`): on first signing use, auto-generate a
  **passphrase-free Ed25519 keypair** at `<tracker>/.opcert-key` / `.opcert-key.pub`
  (`ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -N ""`, key file 0600, git-ignored) — mirroring the HMAC `.signing-key`
  genesis. DSSE principal/keyid defaults to the environment's existing `env_id`;
  `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID` overrides. Verify-side key resolution never creates a key.
- **Plan-review producer**: `sign_plan_review()` builds the same manifest but signs via
  `signing.sign_opcert_manifest(..., material_fingerprint=<material>, merged_log_commit=<verified_at_sha>,
  key_path=<env key>, principal=<env id>)` (kind derived from `manifest[0]` = `plan-review`). The
  never-sign structural guards (non-PASS, degraded coverage) are preserved unchanged.
- **Completion-verifier producer**: the close-gate sign call in `transition_close.py` signs the
  verdict manifest via `sign_opcert_manifest` (kind `completion-verifier`), binding the
  `verified-at-sha` as the cert's commit; the material-drift recheck and the
  `certifiable: false` / `source=="local"` withhold rules are preserved unchanged.
- **Read path (expand)**: `verify_signature` / `plan_review.attest.compute_validity` certify
  envelope-bearing attestations against the environment's own public key (`.opcert-key.pub`) for
  same-environment verification; a cert from another environment surfaces the existing
  `foreign_key`-style non-verified outcome; legacy HMAC records still verify exactly as today
  (removed later by the contract story). Validity-on-read lifecycle rules (reopen, code drift,
  material drift) apply to envelope attestations identically.
- **MCP seam**: the MCP `sign_manifest` tool rides the same repointed path (it calls
  `signing.sign_manifest`'s successor seam), so MCP-signed manifests also become op-certs.
- **Degrade, never wedge**: if `ssh-keygen` (OpenSSH ≥ 8.9, `sshsig.ensure_available()`) is
  unavailable, signing records the existing in-band `{signed: false, error: …}` outcome; no local
  operation is blocked by signing itself. A project whose claim/close gate requires the signature
  then blocks at that gate with a clear install-OpenSSH remediation message.

## Scope
- In: key genesis + principal resolution; repointing both producers; expand-phase read path; MCP
  `sign_manifest` seam; tests below.
- Out: HMAC removal (the contract story, 8f1d); the remote service and its client command (ee0b);
  AWS IaC (the deploy story); any change to gate YAMLs or verdict schemas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] First signing use in a fresh environment generates `<tracker>/.opcert-key`(+`.pub`),
      passphrase-free Ed25519, mode 0600, git-ignored; a test asserts genesis, permissions, and
      that verify-side resolution never creates a key.
- [ ] A passing plan review produces an envelope-bearing `SIGNATURE` event (no HMAC hex) whose
      DSSE subject binds `{ticket_id, material_fingerprint, verified-at sha, kind="plan-review"}`;
      a test asserts `verify_signature(kind="plan-review")` certifies it in the same environment
      and that the never-sign guards (non-PASS / degraded coverage) still refuse to sign.
- [ ] A gated close produces an envelope-bearing `SIGNATURE` event (kind `completion-verifier`)
      via the same seam; a test asserts the material-drift recheck still withholds signing on
      drift and `certifiable: false` still closes unsigned.
- [ ] Expand-phase read-both: a test asserts a legacy HMAC attestation written before this change
      still verifies/reads as today, alongside a new envelope attestation on the same ticket
      (kind-keyed coexistence per the additive attestations model).
- [ ] `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID` overrides the DSSE principal; absent, the principal equals the
      environment's `env_id`; a test asserts both.
- [ ] With `ssh-keygen` unavailable (patched `ensure_available`), a plan-review PASS records the
      in-band `{signed: false, …}` outcome and the operation exits successfully; a test asserts
      the claim gate then reports the missing-attestation block with a remediation that names
      OpenSSH ≥ 8.9.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green.
data.title: op-cert: producer signing seam — gates mint Ed25519 op-certs with the environment key (expand phase)
